key_insight: A hybrid GNN-LSTM surrogate can learn how local microstructure topology and loading history determine the static stress-strain response of additively manufactured short-fiber thermoplastics.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, physics-informed prediction of stiffness and damage-prone regions in SFT components, supporting digital twins and design screening for lightweight aerospace and automotive parts.
method: Reconstruct microstructures from micro-CT and partition them into Voronoi-based cells representing fiber-interaction neighborhoods.
Run nonlinear cell-level FE homogenization with matrix damage to generate stress-strain training data.
Train a GNN-LSTM to encode microstructural connectivity and history-dependent mechanical evolution.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matches high-fidelity FE results with about R^2 ≈ 0.98 and reduces computational cost by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and representativeness of the reconstructed microstructures and calibrated damage laws, so extrapolation to unseen manufacturing conditions may be limited.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
